images.desc: Smooth, human-like control with real-time gravity compensation.
Works with MuJoCo and Isaac Sim for realistic testing and reinforcement learning.
Compliant, backdrivable joints enable safe, practical human-centered interaction.
Two-way force feedback for contact-rich teleoperation and real-world data collection.

features.title: Key Features
features.subtitle: Core features accelerating innovation from research to mass production

items.title: Bimanual System
items.desc: Perfectly synchronized bimanual 14DOF control enables human-level complex task execution.

items.title: 1kHz CAN-FD Control
items.desc: High-speed communication protocol developed based on CAN-FD realizes ultra-precise real-time motion control.

items.title: Affordable Platform
items.desc: An open-source-based affordable price dramatically lowers the barrier to entry for research and industrial applications.

items.title: Lightweight & Powerful
items.desc: Despite its light 5.5kg arm weight, it boasts a powerful peak payload of up to 6kg.

items.title: Open Source Ecosystem
items.desc: Fully compatible with ROS2 and supported by a rich open-source community for rapid development.

items.title: Built for AI
items.desc: Provides a hardware architecture optimized for deploying Sim-to-Real reinforcement learning models.

products_page.subtitle: The moment vision becomes reality. OpenArm Official Integration Kit.

faq.subtitle: Here are the most common questions about the OpenArm robotic arm.

items.question: How is OpenArm different from existing industrial robotic arms?
items.answer: OpenArm is the ultimate starting machine to experience collaborative robots at a reasonable price. Unlike expensive industrial robots, we eliminated price bubbles while keeping research-grade specifications. It is comprehensively designed based on open-source so anyone can easily access and modify it, making it optimized for education, research, and prototyping.

items.question: Can I control it directly using Python or ROS?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely! OpenArm provides a Python API and can be directly coded and controlled in various environments including C++, ROS, and WebSockets. It is the best platform for robotics students or AI researchers who want to quickly deploy deep learning and reinforcement learning (RL) models onto a physical robot arm.

items.question: Is it suitable for university labs or high school robotics courses?
items.answer: It is the best choice. With its compact size and light weight, you can safely experiment even in tight spaces. Thanks to its intuitive teaching pendant functionality and open-source nature, it can be utilized in all environments from basic coding education to advanced robotics dynamics research.

items.question: Can I attach accessories like grippers or cameras?
items.answer: Yes, it supports a plug-and-play ecosystem. You can easily adapt dedicated End Effectors like suction pumps and 2-finger grippers, as well as optional high-performance cameras (like the Intel RealSense D405), to easily implement autonomous pick-and-place tasks based on Vision AI.

items.question: How does after-sales and technical support work?
items.answer: You can receive fast and reliable supply and technical support within Korea through Libertron Co., Ltd. We can issue tax invoices suitable for your purpose without complicated import procedures, and we support the first step of introducing a new machine most easily with our door-to-door delivery service. (Delivery services are a local service only supported in Korea.)
